title: Winey Peruvian Rice Noodles Causa Limena
description: A wine-infused, vegan Peruvian dish featuring rice stick noodles shaped like nigiri, wrapped in lotus leaf, and crowned with crumbly toasted almond crumbs.
instructions: Rinse the rice noodles under cold water, then soak them in hot water for 5 minutes until pliable; drain and set aside.
Heat a nonstick skillet over medium heat, add olive oil, and stir in smoked paprika and cumin, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add white wine to the skillet, let it simmer for 2 minutes to reduce slightly, then stir in lime juice.
Toss the soaked noodles into the skillet, coating them evenly with the sauce, and cook for 1 to 2 minutes until heated through.
Remove the skillet from heat, season the noodles with salt, and transfer them to a clean surface to sprinkle toasted almond crumbs while still warm.
Let the coated noodles cool until they are firm enough to hold together but still pliable.
Using clean hands, press each noodle strand into a tight cylinder, shaping it like a small sushi nigiri.
Place each shaped noodle cylinder onto a folded lotus leaf, drizzle a thin line of sauce over it, and wrap the leaf around to create a neat parcel.
